skill.name: skill-check
skill.description: Comprehensive testing and validation of Claude skills. Use this skill whenever the user wants to test, validate, audit, or quality-check a skill — whether they say "test my skill", "check this skill works", "validate my skill", "run skill-check", or anything similar. Also trigger when the user asks things like "does this skill handle edge cases?", "what's wrong with my skill?", "find problems in my skill", "is this skill secure?", "does my skill follow best practices?", or "how robust is this skill?". Even if they just say "check this" and point at a skill directory, use skill-check. This skill covers triggering tests, output quality tests, input/output contract validation, script execution checks, security review, best practices auditing, and multi-file skill analysis. Do NOT use for creating or editing skills (that's skill-creator) — skill-check is purely about testing and reporting on existing skills.
skill.summary: Test, validate, and audit Claude skills for quality, security, and best practices compliance
skill.author: JckJhns

security_audit.summary: SkillCheck is legitimate testing infrastructure for Claude skills. Static findings flagged test fixtures containing intentionally vulnerable code (eval, os.system, hardcoded API keys) and example documentation. The skill correctly uses Bash and filesystem tools to run tests on user-provided skill directories - this is expected behavior for a testing/auditing skill. No evidence of malicious intent. Test fixtures are clearly labeled and isolated.

content.user_title: Test and validate your Claude skills
content.value_statement: Skill authors need reliable testing before publishing. SkillCheck runs comprehensive audits on skill directories to verify functionality, catch security issues, and validate best practices compliance.
actual_capabilities: Run Quick Check for fast static analysis of skill structure and security
Run Standard Check including script execution and I/O contract validation
Run Deep Check for edge cases, end-to-end workflows, and stress testing
Detect security vulnerabilities including shell injection, credential exposure, and path traversal
Validate SKILL.md quality including trigger phrases, description completeness, and constraint consistency
Check best practices compliance across 22 criteria covering spec compliance, structure, writing quality, and efficiency
limitations: Cannot execute scripts that require specific production credentials or live API endpoints
Mock file generation for binary formats requires optional Python packages that may not be available
End-to-end tests on Claude.ai run sequentially in shared context, which may influence later test results

use_cases.title: Pre-publication validation
use_cases.description: Run a Quick Check before publishing to catch structural issues, security concerns, and best practices violations early.

use_cases.title: Debugging broken skills
use_cases.description: Run a Standard Check to identify why a skill fails, using script execution and I/O testing to pinpoint exact failure points.

use_cases.title: Comprehensive quality audit
use_cases.description: Run a Deep Check for thorough validation including edge cases, multi-file analysis, and end-to-end workflow simulation.

prompt_templates.title: Quick validation check
prompt_templates.prompt: Run a Quick Check on my skill at [path]
prompt_templates.scenario: Use this when you need fast feedback on skill structure and potential issues

prompt_templates.title: Debug failing skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Run a Standard Check on [path] and explain why the scripts might be failing
prompt_templates.scenario: Use this when a skill is not working as expected

prompt_templates.title: Security review request
prompt_templates.prompt: Run a Quick Check focused on security issues for [path]
prompt_templates.scenario: Use this when you want to focus specifically on potential security vulnerabilities

prompt_templates.title: Complete quality audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Run a Deep Check on [path] including edge cases
prompt_templates.scenario: Use this when preparing a skill for production or after major changes

output_examples.input: Run a Quick Check on the skill at ./my-skill/
output_examples.output:
Summary: MOSTLY HEALTHY (2 warnings)

Security findings: 0 Critical, 1 Warning
Best practices score: 18/22

Warnings:
- Description missing negative guidance (when NOT to use this skill)

- SKILL.md exceeds 200 lines; consider progressive disclosure

output_examples.input: Run a Standard Check on the skill at ./data-processor/
output_examples.output:
Summary: NEEDS ATTENTION (3 failures)


Failed tests:
1. Cross-reference mismatch: FIXTURE.md uses --file but script expects --input
2. Script execution failed: Missing dependency 'python-magic'
3. I/O contract mismatch: Output includes timestamps but spec does not mention them

Security: No critical issues found

best_practices: Provide specific file paths or skill directory locations for faster analysis
Specify the check depth (Quick/Standard/Deep) based on your needs and time constraints
Review the improvement suggestions in priority order: Critical issues must be fixed before lower-priority items
anti_patterns: Asking SkillCheck to create or edit skills - use skill-creator for that instead
Running Deep Check on incomplete skills with many TODOs - use Quick Check and frame results as a progress report
Ignoring Critical security findings and running Standard/Deep checks anyway - security issues should be resolved first

faq.question: What is the difference between Quick, Standard, and Deep checks?
faq.answer: Quick checks run static analysis only - no scripts are executed. Standard checks add script execution and I/O validation. Deep checks include edge cases, end-to-end workflows, and stress testing.

faq.question: When should I use Quick Check versus Standard Check?
faq.answer: Use Quick Check for fast feedback during development or before publishing. Use Standard Check when you need to verify scripts actually work or validate input/output contracts.

faq.question: Can SkillCheck fix issues it finds?
faq.answer: No, SkillCheck only tests and reports findings. Use skill-creator or edit files manually to fix issues.

faq.question: What does the security review check for?
faq.answer: It scans scripts for shell injection risks, credential exposure, arbitrary code execution, path traversal vulnerabilities, and dangerous instruction patterns in SKILL.md.

faq.question: Why did the skill find issues in my test fixtures?
faq.answer: If you have intentionally insecure test files, they will be flagged. Move test fixtures to a separate directory outside the skill being tested if you want to avoid false positives.

faq.question: How accurate are the best practices scores?
faq.answer: Best practices are advisory, not pass/fail. A skill can be functionally perfect but score lower on best practices. Use the score to identify improvement opportunities, not as a pass/fail gate.
